slides.title: Delegation Maintenance Automation Status Extension for EPP
RDAP Extension for Structured Reliability Assessment Metadata

polls.text: Do you feel comfortable enough to discuss generic provisioning (not EPP/variants) process issues? (Yes/No)

chat.author: Richard Wilhelm
chat.text: Notes doc is open and (as usual) pre-populated with the agenda, links, etc.  Feel free to jump in and assist if you wish!

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: I was too slow to get to the mic. What's the likely time-line to get those three IDs out the door?

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: OK - thanks Antoin

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: I think the URL for Mario's document is wrong in the slides, it should be https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-regext-rdap-jscontact/

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: It's also on the list twice.

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: cut and paster error?

chat.author: Richard Wilhelm
chat.text: I think you are right Gavin

chat.author: Richard Wilhelm
chat.text: thx for the new URL... it's fixed in the Notes

chat.author: James Galvin
chat.text: Thanks Gavin.  I'll fix that in the record.

chat.author: James Galvin
chat.text: And thanks Ruth.  I'll fix the duplicate too.

chat.author: James Galvin
chat.text: :joy:

chat.author: James Galvin
chat.text: The official record of slides has been fixed

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: it's the SOA at the end of the XFR that tells you that you got a complete set of records :)

chat.author: Richard Wilhelm
chat.text: ROFL

chat.author: Paweł Kowalik
chat.text: would the chairs also show the documents in the adoption queue atm please? I think those are missing from the full picture between existing and new work.

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: https://github.com/ietf-wg-regext

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: I like github

chat.author: Mohamed Boucadair
chat.text: @Pawel, I see at least two I-Ds tagged as "Candidate for WG Adoption" in the datatracker but I'm not sure this captures all such cases. @Chairs: please confirm/infirm.

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: @Ruth, you are a bad lady - a very bad lady. :-)

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: @jim I'll go further - let's ditch email and do all our work in github and slack. I woke up in a spicy mood.

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: My head has exploded. :grinning:

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: that just sounds like database design

chat.author: James Gould
chat.text: It sounds like business logic to me

chat.author: Jody Kolker
chat.text: Other cctlds have done this so it seems we have at least examples to review and can list the pros and cons.

chat.author: Mohamed Boucadair
chat.text: The first doc listed by Gavin will be published as RFC10026

chat.author: Mohamed Boucadair
chat.text: and as BCP246

chat.author: James Galvin
chat.text: @pawel - there are two documents listed as candidates for adoption and that is correct.

chat.author: James Galvin
chat.text: In order that is the verified contacts draft and then the eku draft

chat.author: James Galvin
chat.text: Note that as an operational practice, REGEXT limits itself to 10 documents on the agenda, which we have.  So, these two docs we are discussing today could go on the list, when requested by the authors, but they all will be waiting until some other document drops off our list before they can be adopted.

chat.author: Duane Wessels
chat.text: can I get a delegAutomation?

chat.author: James Galvin
chat.text: The need to adopt some new work should be motivation for getting existing work off our agenda, i.e., rdap extensions, versioning, x-media-type.

chat.author: Peter Thomassen
chat.text: hehe Duane. I guess it's extensible now. :) (Before Gavin added nsAutomation for CSYNC, it was just "automation" which was not extensible.)

chat.author: Kristian Ørmen
chat.text: How would this work if a domain is transferred to another registrar. Does the setting follow the domain over to the new registrar?

chat.author: Duane Wessels
chat.text: I'm a fan of extensible extensions

chat.author: Suzanne Woolf
chat.text: Amen @Duane :-)

chat.author: Richard Wilhelm
chat.text: @Kristian:  I think that the setting would need to be re-applied

chat.author: Richard Wilhelm
chat.text: Registry Lock is a product subscription (+1 to Ulrich's comment)

chat.author: Kristian Ørmen
chat.text: @Richard yeah, i think we would need to reset it as well. For a long time way to many registrars would not be aware of the setting if it come to them in a transfer

chat.author: Peter Thomassen
chat.text: Gavin: I heard somewhere this extension would allow a registrar to disable automation for all sponsored domains. How?

chat.author: Kristian Ørmen
chat.text: It would be interesting with a setting to disable scanning on a host record

chat.author: Peter Thomassen
chat.text: (to my Q: for each domain individually, or would there be a more generic way?)

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: The orthogonal point caught my attention. Does that mean that if this is applied, it would also affect serverUpdateProhibited set for other reasons and allow registrar to control automation behaviour while the domain is locked for other reasons (URS etc)?

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: Peter: this extension would not do that. Most registries provide a web interface to allow registrars to configure global options for their account

chat.author: Peter Thomassen
chat.text: got it, thx

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: I don't know if that's actually a problem, but it feels wrong somehow

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: Ruth: but this extension would not allow a client to override server policy. A server could still stop scanning a domain subject to a URS lock even if the client had enabled it.

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: Gotcha. Ok, thanks.

chat.author: Peter Thomassen
chat.text: for URS, I think it's interesting to consider whether DS (not NS) automation should continue if previously enabled. I think it should as during URS Lock the domain should remain accessible, and DS automation is necessary for performing required maintenance.

chat.author: Peter Thomassen
chat.text: Analogy: if a web site were frozen, one probably would still allow certificate renewals to keep it accessible.

chat.author: Ruth Trevor-Allen
chat.text: Yep, but it'd still be server policy I think?

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: if it's not being run by a registry or registrar, why is it in RDAP?

chat.author: James Gould
chat.text: So a seperate provider is running an RDAP service for this purpose.

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: I don't think this is work for regext.

chat.author: Jody Kolker
chat.text: +1 Gavin - I was wondering where this goes.

chat.author: Maarten Wullink
chat.text: that makes more sense

chat.author: Jim Reid
chat.text: If not here Gavin, where?

chat.author: James Gould
chat.text: Interesting that is an RDAP extension for a different provider.  Technically the work would belong in REGEXT, but maybe not based on the charter.

chat.author: Richard Wilhelm
chat.text: In RDAP so it's easy to query with standard tools.  Far better than a non-standard API for domain-oriented stuff

chat.author: James Gould
chat.text: RDAP can certainly be used by other providers than registries and registrars.

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: so would this other RDAP server mirror the other RDAP response elements from the registry/registrar, and enrich it? or would it just be the ldhName and extension?

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: and how exactly is an RDAP client going to learn about this service, and how can they use it in the context of a standard RDAP request?

chat.author: James Gould
chat.text: Good question, since its extending the registrar (entity) and domain objects.

chat.author: James Gould
chat.text: It would most likely need a seperate bootstrap if one is needed at all.

chat.author: Richard Wilhelm
chat.text:
I don't think that it fits into the bootstrap... because it's not Authoritiative.
User would need to "know" where it is

chat.author: James Galvin
chat.text: As an extension to RDAP, if the desire is to standardize the extension, then it belongs in REGEXT.  Certainly it could be documented and put in the IANA registry as a proprietary extension, separate from REGEXT.

chat.author: Gavin Brown
chat.text: thank you chairs and scribe(s)!
